task_completion.description: The Gazebo object is located and selected in the software's object library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The object catalog or library panel is open, and a Gazebo is selected, highlighted, or being prepared for placement.

task_completion.description: A Gazebo is placed in the backyard area of the property.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main canvas (in 2D or 3D view) shows a Gazebo structure located in the yard behind the house.

task_completion.description: The Gazebo is placed on grass or open ground and does not overlap with other structures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Gazebo is positioned entirely on the green grass/ground area. It does not clip into or overlap with the house walls, fences, trees, or other objects.

task_completion.description: The project is saved after placing the Gazebo.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The File menu is open with 'Save Project' highlighted, a save dialog box is visible, or a save confirmation is shown on screen.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with NCH DreamPlan Home Design Software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The DreamPlan UI is visible, not a generic image viewer, web browser, or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent used the GUI to place the object and save the project.
integrity.visual_evidence: Mouse cursor interacting with the DreamPlan menus, object library, and canvas.

integrity.description: The Gazebo appears as a rendered 3D/2D object within the software's canvas.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Gazebo matches the rendering style of the software and integrates with the scene (e.g., shadows, perspective), rather than looking like an image pasted over the screenshot.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task is performed in NCH DreamPlan Home Design Software v9.28 on Windows 11. The starting project is the 'Contemporary House' sample project. The target location is the Backyard, which is the area behind the house. The project should be saved, typically to 'Documents\DreamPlan Projects'.
